named_technique: Sekanina counting + centered-hexagonal differences + finite modular obstruction (mod 7, 9, or 13)
bridge_lemma: If A ⊂ Z is an exact unique-representation complement of {n^3 : n > 0}, then the cubic difference identity f(n+1) − f(n) = 3n^2 + 3n + 1 (centered hexagonal) forces a quantitative modular density constraint on A modulo some small prime power m ∈ {7, 9, 13, 21, 63}; for at least one such m the resulting count of pairs (a + n^3) covering each residue class either undershoots or overshoots, contradicting uniqueness.
informal_proof_outline: Sekanina (1959) closed the X^2 case by exploiting f(n+1)−f(n) = 2n+1, which enumerates every odd positive integer exactly once. Combined with a mod-4 residue argument on (a, n^2) pairs and the density |A ∩ [-N,N]| ~ √N forced by uniqueness, this yields a finite counting contradiction. For X^3, the difference identity becomes f(n+1)−f(n) = 3n^2+3n+1, the centered hexagonal numbers (1, 7, 19, 37, 61, ...), all congruent to 1 mod 6. The cubic differences are sparser than odd integers, so the Sekanina template does not transfer literally — but a parallel finite modular obstruction is the natural candidate. Cubes mod m have restricted residue support: mod 7 cubes ∈ {0,1,6}, mod 9 cubes ∈ {0,1,8}, mod 13 cubes ∈ {0,1,5,8,12}. The uniqueness of z = a + n^3 mod m means that for each z mod m, the count of (a mod m, n^3 mod m) pairs covering z is exactly the count of (a mod m) values, weighted by the cube-residue multiplicity. Density: |{n^3 : n ≥ 1} ∩ [1,N]| ~ N^{1/3} forces |A ∩ [-N,N]| ~ N^{2/3}. The information-theoretic count of A mod m must satisfy a finite linear system over each modulus; for at least one m ∈ {7, 9, 13, 21, 63} this system is OVERDETERMINED and inconsistent, giving the contradiction. Aristotle's informal reasoner should attempt the modular search systematically: for each m enumerate cube residues mod m, compute the implied A-residue distribution, and check whether the uniqueness count is satisfiable. If satisfiable for all small m, falsify and report which modular structure permits uniqueness (this would be a publishable disprove-of-the-modular-route partial result). Falsification (X^3 case has a unique-representation complement) would itself be a major advance.
candidate_lemmas: L1_difference_identity: for f(n) = n^3, f(n+1) − f(n) = 3n^2 + 3n + 1 ∈ Z for all n; image is the centered hexagonal sequence.
L2_centered_hexagonal_mod_6: every centered hexagonal number is congruent to 1 mod 6, so the set of cubic forward-differences lies in a single residue class mod 6.
L3_cube_residue_mod_7: cubes mod 7 take exactly three residues, namely {0, 1, 6}; equivalently n^3 ≡ 0, 1, or −1 mod 7 for every n.
L4_density_lower_bound: if A is a unique-representation complement of {n^3 : n > 0} in Z, then |A ∩ [-N, N]| ~ N^{2/3} as N → ∞.

L5_uniqueness_to_modular_count: uniqueness of z = a + n^3 implies that for every modulus m and every residue r mod m, the count |{(a, n) : a ∈ A, n > 0, a + n^3 ≡ r mod m, a ∈ [-N, N], n^3 ≤ N}| equals (1 + o(1)) · N / m as N → ∞.

L6_finite_modular_obstruction: for some m ∈ {7, 9, 13, 21, 63} the linear system from L5 combined with the cube-residue support {0, 1, 6} (m=7), {0, 1, 8} (m=9), or {0, 1, 5, 8, 12} (m=13) is inconsistent for the X^3 case but consistent for X^2, isolating the cubic-specific obstruction.
L7_main_theorem: combining L1–L6, no A ⊂ Z can satisfy the unique-representation condition for f(n) = n^3.
honest_disclaimer: W4-D dossier (May 31 2026, Claude Opus 4.7 1M-context). HONEST: this is the FUSION-lane attempt on E477 X^3 after the existing slot 747 CLOSURE-lane submission (UUID 7c9dd7d8) remains pending without result. The Sekanina-style modular obstruction is the AUTHOR'S analysis, not a paired-LLM-validated strategy. Gemini quota exhausted during W4-D consultation (12h reset). Grok-4.3 round 1 claimed 'Ruzsa proved no exact complement of cubes in the 1990s' — VERIFIED HALLUCINATED against arXiv:1510.00812 abstract (which only refines Sárközy-Szemerédi covering bounds, not unique-representation). arXiv:2512.15407 (2026) proves the closest related result for r-th-power EXACT-ON-AVERAGE complements (Cilleruelo 1993 conjecture for r=2) — strictly weaker than the unique-representation X^3 case demanded by E477. fit_score = 0.45 reflects HONEST uncertainty: the modular search space is finite per modulus and Aristotle can enumerate it, but no published result rules in or rules out a small-modulus obstruction for cubes. Plausibility of compiled_advance: ~0.10. Plausibility of compiled_no_advance: ~0.55. Plausibility of falsification (Aristotle finds the obstruction does NOT exist mod small m and identifies which modular structure permits uniqueness): ~0.20. This dossier accepts that paired-LLM consultation FAILED to validate the strategy; the FUSION lane is being used to invoke Aristotle's informal reasoner directly with the modular obstruction as a starting point. paired_llm = 'grok-4-1-fast-reasoning + WebSearch (Gemini quota exhausted)'.
